Package global.thalion.ttio

Class SpectralDataset

java.lang.Object
global.thalion.ttio.SpectralDataset
All Implemented Interfaces:
Encryptable, AutoCloseable
public class SpectralDataset extends Object implements Encryptable, AutoCloseable
Root reader/writer for TTI-O .tio files. Implements AutoCloseable for try-with-resources.

HDF5 layout: root group has @ttio_format_version and @ttio_features attributes. The /study/ group contains runs, identifications, quantifications, and provenance.

Compound metadata (§6 of format-spec). Writing emits the native HDF5 compound dataset and a JSON attribute mirror (transition-window behavior, §6.4). Reading prefers the JSON attribute because JHI5 1.10 cannot marshal VL-string fields out of a compound; when only the native compound is present, primitive fields are recovered via type projection and VL-string fields decode as empty strings. The mirror is emitted to keep Java-written files fully round-trippable by every implementation.

API status: Stable. Encryptable conformance is delivered in slice 41.5 when the encryption manager lands in Java.

Cross-language equivalents: Objective-C TTIOSpectralDataset, Python ttio.spectral_dataset.SpectralDataset.

Since:
0.6

  • Method Details

    • filePath

      public String filePath()
      The absolute path of the underlying .tio file (null for in-memory datasets).

    • provider

      public StorageProvider provider()
      M39: the owning storage provider. New call sites should reach for this instead of the native Hdf5File.

    • isEncrypted

      public boolean isEncrypted()
      true iff this dataset carries an @encrypted root attribute. Survives close/reopen because the value is read back from disk by open(java.lang.String). Mirrors Python SpectralDataset.is_encrypted and Objective-C -[TTIOSpectralDataset isEncrypted].

    • encryptedAlgorithm

      public String encryptedAlgorithm()
      Algorithm string stored in the root @encrypted attribute, or the empty string when the dataset is not encrypted. Typical value is "aes-256-gcm". Mirrors Python SpectralDataset.encrypted_algorithm and Objective-C -[TTIOSpectralDataset encryptedAlgorithm].

    • open

      public static SpectralDataset open(String pathOrUrl)
      Open an existing .tio file for reading. v0.9 M64.5 (Java): a URL scheme (memory://, sqlite://, zarr://) dispatches to the matching StorageProvider and reads the whole dataset through the protocol (StorageGroup-based); bare paths (and file:// URLs) stay on the HDF5 fast path for byte parity with pre-M64.5 files and the cross-language smoke suite.

    • create

      public static SpectralDataset create(String path, String title, String isaInvestigationId, List<AcquisitionRun> runs, List<Identification> identifications, List<Quantification> quantifications, List<ProvenanceRecord> provenanceRecords)
      Create a new .tio file with the given content.

    • create

      public static SpectralDataset create(String pathOrUrl, String title, String isaInvestigationId, List<AcquisitionRun> runs, List<Identification> identifications, List<Quantification> quantifications, List<ProvenanceRecord> provenanceRecords, FeatureFlags featureFlags)

    • encryptWithKey

      public void encryptWithKey(byte[] key, Enums.EncryptionLevel level) throws Exception
      Description copied from interface: Encryptable
      Encrypt this object's protectable content at the given granularity.
      Specified by:
      encryptWithKey in interface Encryptable
      Parameters:
      key - raw key material; length and format are algorithm-specific
      level - the granularity at which to apply protection
      Throws:
      Exception - if encryption fails (key material invalid, I/O error, etc.)

    • decryptWithKey

      public void decryptWithKey(byte[] key) throws Exception
      Description copied from interface: Encryptable
      Decrypt previously-encrypted content.
      Specified by:
      decryptWithKey in interface Encryptable
      Parameters:
      key - raw key material matching the key used to encrypt
      Throws:
      Exception - if decryption fails (wrong key, data corrupt, etc.)

    • decryptInPlace

      public static void decryptInPlace(String path, byte[] key)
      v1.1.1: persist-to-disk decrypt. Strips AES-256-GCM encryption from the .tio file at path: for every MS run with an encrypted intensity channel, writes plaintext back as intensity_values and removes the encrypted siblings. Finally clears the root @encrypted attribute so isEncrypted() returns false when the file is reopened.

      Symmetric with encryptWithKey(byte[], global.thalion.ttio.Enums.EncryptionLevel) (which leaves the root attribute set). After this call the file is byte-compatible with the pre-encryption layout.

      The file must not be held open by another writer.

      Cross-language equivalents: Python SpectralDataset.decrypt_in_place, Objective-C +[TTIOSpectralDataset decryptInPlaceAtPath:withKey:error:].
